messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following corporate travel management platform privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"TravelDesk processes employee names, passport details, travel itineraries, hotel bookings, and expense records to manage corporate travel on behalf of your employer. We act as a data processor; your employer is the data controller. Processing occurs only on employer instructions. Data is stored in AWS EU (Ireland). Records are retained per your employer's instructions or 5 years by default. We assist your employer in responding to data subject requests. Our DPO is at dpo@traveldesk.com. Sub-processors are listed at traveldesk.com/subprocessors. We notify the employer within 24 hours of a confirmed breach. We require all sub-processors to sign DPAs meeting GDPR standards."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Correctly structured as a processor. Lawful basis responsibility sits with the employer as controller. Processing on instructions per Art. 28.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Passport details can reveal nationality and potentially ethnic origin. Travel itineraries to religious sites or medical facilities can infer special category data. No acknowledgement or enhanced safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Corporate travel management is a clear and specific purpose. Processing limited to employer instructions.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for corporate travel management.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Employer instructions or 5-year default. Clear and reasonable.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Assistance with data subject requests on employer instruction satisfies Art. 28(3)(e).

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Sub-processors listed at a URL; all required to sign DPAs.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] AWS EU Ireland confirms EEA storage.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO at dpo@traveldesk.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 24-hour employer notification stated, exceeding Art. 33 minimum.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (passport and travel destination data may reveal special category information — not addressed)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that passport data and travel itineraries may reveal ethnic origin, religious beliefs, or health information and state appropriate safeguards or handling procedures
